skill.name: chronicle-session-documenter
skill.description: Create structured Obsidian notes from Chronicle development session data using MCP tools or Chronicle CLI commands.
skill.summary: Documents AI development sessions as searchable Obsidian notes with metadata, summaries, and links.
skill.author: ChandlerHardy

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command and filesystem patterns, but most are markdown examples for legitimate Chronicle and Obsidian workflows. The confirmed risk is that the skill instructs an AI agent to run local Chronicle CLI commands and write files to an Obsidian vault, so it should be published with a clear capability warning.

medium_findings.title: Agent Can Run Local Chronicle Commands and Write Notes
medium_findings.description: The skill tells the agent to call Chronicle and Obsidian MCP tools, run Chronicle CLI commands, and create or overwrite Obsidian notes. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it grants filesystem-changing behavior and local command execution that users should understand.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly instruct use of MCP tool calls, Chronicle CLI commands, and vault file writes. The behavior is expected for the skill, but the command and filesystem capability is real.

low_findings.title: Static Path Traversal Findings Are Benign Examples
low_findings.description: The flagged path text is a placeholder repository path and a tip for extracting a repository name from a path. No evidence found of path traversal logic, untrusted path concatenation, or attempts to escape a controlled directory.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both lines are documentation examples showing path-shaped strings. They do not implement file access or accept user-controlled traversal sequences.

low_findings.title: Temporary File Example Has Limited Local Exposure
low_findings.description: The CLI example redirects Chronicle output to a temporary file. This is a local workflow pattern, but users should avoid storing sensitive session details in shared temporary locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line explicitly writes session data to a temporary path, but it is an example command and not hidden or remote behavior. Risk depends on the user environment and session sensitivity.

low_findings.title: Static Blocker and Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged weak cryptography and reconnaissance signals come from ordinary documentation text and local session metadata handling. No evidence found of cryptographic operations, host probing, credential discovery, or environment enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is descriptive markdown and local note handling. There are no commands or instructions that perform system reconnaissance or weak cryptographic operations.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command and MCP Tool Invocation Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes documented calls to Chronicle MCP methods, Obsidian MCP write methods, and Chronicle CLI commands. These are not malicious, but they are capability-bearing instructions that can read local session data and write notes.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The lines are explicit tool and command usage instructions. They match the stated purpose and do not show malicious intent, but they require user trust.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Write Guidance for Obsidian Vault Notes
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the agent to create notes under Chronicle session paths and use overwrite mode in an Obsidian write operation. This can modify user files and should be disclosed.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file write behavior is directly documented, including overwrite mode. It is scoped to Obsidian notes, with no evidence of stealthy or destructive file operations.

content.value_statement: Development sessions are hard to find after the work is finished. This skill turns Chronicle session data into structured Obsidian notes with metadata, summaries, and links.
actual_capabilities: Retrieves Chronicle session summaries through MCP when available.
Falls back to Chronicle CLI commands for portable session lookup.
Formats session notes with frontmatter, summaries, accomplishments, files, and related links.
Writes notes to an Obsidian vault through an Obsidian MCP tool when available.
Provides patterns for documenting one session, recent sessions, or sessions matching a topic.
limitations: Requires Chronicle data to exist before it can document a session.
CLI output must be parsed manually or by the assistant when MCP is unavailable.
Obsidian writing requires an available Obsidian MCP tool or manual file creation.
Recent sessions may not have generated summaries immediately after completion.

use_cases.title: Create End-of-Session Notes
use_cases.description: Turn a completed Chronicle session into a durable Obsidian note with summary, decisions, files, and links.

use_cases.title: Build a Searchable Engineering Journal
use_cases.description: Document multiple recent sessions so future work can be searched and linked inside an Obsidian vault.

use_cases.title: Prepare Handoff Context
use_cases.description: Capture session outcomes and blockers before handing work to another engineer or another AI coding tool.

prompt_templates.title: Document One Session
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a known session ID.
prompt_templates.prompt: Document Chronicle session 10 in my Obsidian vault. Include the summary, changed files, decisions, blockers, and related links.

prompt_templates.title: Find Recent Sessions
prompt_templates.scenario: User does not know the session ID.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find my recent Chronicle sessions from today and help me choose which ones should be documented in Obsidian.

prompt_templates.title: Document a Topic
prompt_templates.scenario: Search by work area before creating notes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Chronicle for sessions about authentication, then create Obsidian notes for the most relevant sessions.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Linked Session Archive
prompt_templates.prompt: Document the last 20 Chronicle development sessions in Obsidian. Use consistent frontmatter, wikilinks, repository links, and commit references where available.

output_examples.input: Document session 10 to my Obsidian vault.
output_examples.output: A session note is created with title, duration, repository, tool name, summary, accomplishments, decisions, modified files, blockers, and related links.

output_examples.input: Find sessions about authentication and document them.
output_examples.output: The assistant searches Chronicle, selects matching sessions, and creates linked Obsidian notes for the relevant development work.
The assistant lists today's Chronicle sessions, skips incomplete entries when needed, and creates a consistent note for each selected session.

best_practices: Review the target note path before allowing overwrite operations.
Prefer MCP summary data when available because it is more structured than CLI output.
Wait a few minutes after a long session so Chronicle summary generation can finish.
anti_patterns: Do not document sessions that may contain secrets without reviewing the generated note.
Do not overwrite manually edited Obsidian notes without user confirmation.
Do not assume CLI parsing is complete when session output is missing sections.

faq.question: Does this skill require MCP tools?
faq.answer: No. MCP is preferred for structured data, but the skill also supports Chronicle CLI commands.

faq.question: Can it write directly to Obsidian?
faq.answer: Yes, if an Obsidian MCP write tool is available. Otherwise, it guides manual note creation.

faq.question: What data goes into each note?
faq.answer: Each note can include metadata, summary, accomplishments, decisions, changed files, blockers, and related links.

faq.question: Can it document more than one session?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes patterns for documenting recent sessions or sessions found by search.

faq.question: Will it overwrite existing notes?
faq.answer: The examples include overwrite mode, so users should review existing notes or ask for confirmation first.

faq.question: Is this safe for sensitive work?
faq.answer: Use care. Chronicle summaries may contain project details, file paths, or other sensitive development context.
